fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 85 uses a strict greater-than comparison (>), causing items to be considered valid when the current time is exactly equal to their TTL. It should use a greater-than-or-equal-to comparison (>=) to correctly expire items at their TTL boundary.

constraints.intent: If the item is considered valid (i.e., the expiry check fails), the current time must be strictly less than its TTL.
An item is considered valid only if the current time is strictly before its TTL.
A payload should only be returned if its TTL has not yet been reached.

spec.expr: (__cbfl_result is not None) ==> (key.strip() in cache and now < cache[key.strip()][1] + _CLOCK_SKEW_TOLERANCE)

constraints.intent: If a non-None payload is returned, it must correspond to a valid, unexpired entry in the cache.
